ferx 0.1.5
  • Reference
  • Articles
  • Changelog

    Structured warnings from a ferx fit

    ferx_warnings.Rd

    Returns or prints the warnings produced by a fit, classified by severity (critical, warning, info) and category. Severity and category are assigned by the ferx-core engine (for engine warnings) or by the R diagnostics layer (for condition number and ETA normality); the R side never re-parses message text to guess severity.

    ferx_warnings(fit, as_df = FALSE)

    Arguments

    fit

    A ferx_fit object returned by ferx_fit.

    as_df

    Logical. When TRUE, returns the raw data frame (severity, category, message, source_method) instead of pretty-printing. Default FALSE.

    Value

    When as_df = TRUE, a data frame. Otherwise the same data frame invisibly, after printing a grouped, colour-coded summary (critical first, then warning, then info).

    See also

    Other diagnostics: check_diagnostics(), ferx_cor_matrix(), ferx_cov_screen(), ferx_estimates(), ferx_eta_cov(), ferx_plot_trace(), ferx_runlog_iters(), ferx_trace(), summary.ferx_fit()

    Examples

    ex  <- ferx_example("warfarin")
fit <- ferx_fit(ex$model, ex$data, method = "gn", covariance = FALSE)
#> Warning: Model file [fit_options] sets `method = foce` but ferx_fit() argument overrides it with `gn`. The call-time value will be used.
#> Warning: Model file [fit_options] sets `covariance = true` but ferx_fit() argument overrides it with `false`. The call-time value will be used.
#> Mu-referencing detected for: ETA_CL, ETA_KA, ETA_V
ferx_warnings(fit)
#> ferx fit warnings  (warfarin)
#> -------------------------------------------------
#> [WARNING]  dw_autocorrelation
#>             Negative IWRES autocorrelation detected (Durbin-Watson = 2.61).
#>             Possible over-parameterization or misspecified error model.
#>             Negative IWRES autocorrelation suggests over-parameterisation or a
#>             misspecified error model. Consider removing a parameter or
#>             simplifying the residual model.
#> 
#> [INFO]     mu_referencing
#>             mu-ref: ETA_CL, ETA_KA, ETA_V
#>             Mu-referencing was auto-detected for the listed parameters
#>             (informational).
#> 
#> -------------------------------------------------
#> 0 CRITICAL   1 WARNING   1 INFO
ferx_warnings(fit, as_df = TRUE)
#>   severity           category
#> 1     info     mu_referencing
#> 2  warning dw_autocorrelation
#>                                                                                                                       message
#> 1                                                                                               mu-ref: ETA_CL, ETA_KA, ETA_V
#> 2 Negative IWRES autocorrelation detected (Durbin-Watson = 2.61). Possible over-parameterization or misspecified error model.
#>   source_method
#> 1              
#> 2
